metadata.source_note: brand-audit.md currently lives at the repo root. Token hexes verified against client/assets/css/main.css and client/tailwind.config.js.
metadata.canonical_green_rationale: Three greens currently compete in the system: ttc-whatsapp (#25D366, hardcoded raw hex in tailwind.config.js:35, no theme variant), ttc-secondary / ttc-success (both route through the CSS variable --color-secondary and theme-shift #25D366 → #1FAD54), and the shopping category (#10B981, hardcoded outside the token system). ttc-secondary is the only candidate already plumbed into the token system with light+dark variants, so it is the only green that can theme-switch today. It is also the canonical semantic 'success / positive amount' color in AmountDisplay. Promoting it to THE brand green unifies success, WhatsApp-adjacent identity, and marketing energy under one token. ttc-whatsapp should remain available for WhatsApp-specific chrome only (CTA buttons, WhatsAppHeroDemo mimicry), but is not the brand green. See marketing-decisions.md decision #2.

tokens.name: ttc-primary
tokens.notes: Brand blue. Always used as the 'the' accent in the wordmark (AppLogo.vue:9) and as primary CTA background. This is the single strongest brand identity color.

tokens.name: ttc-primary-light
tokens.notes: Hover state and gradient partner for ttc-primary. Rarely used alone.

tokens.name: ttc-secondary
tokens.notes: CANONICAL 'our green' (see metadata.canonical_green). Routes through --color-secondary so it theme-switches. Shares dark-mode hex with ttc-whatsapp but is the themeable/brand-owned token.

tokens.name: ttc-whatsapp
tokens.notes: Hardcoded raw hex in tailwind.config.js:35 — bypasses the CSS variable system, does NOT theme-switch. Reserve for WhatsApp-specific chrome only (WhatsApp CTA in LandingHero.vue:28, beta badge LandingHero.vue:7, WhatsAppHeroDemo). For brand-green usage, use ttc-secondary instead. Shares dark hex with ttc-secondary.

tokens.name: ttc-success
tokens.notes: Semantic alias of ttc-secondary (both route through different CSS vars but resolve to identical hexes). Use for success toasts, positive amounts in AmountDisplay, and confirmation states.

tokens.name: ttc-gold
tokens.notes: Alias of ttc-secondary via tailwind.config.js:36. Name is legacy/confusing — the color is green, not gold. Avoid in new code; prefer ttc-secondary.

tokens.name: ttc-accent
tokens.notes: Warm coral-red accent. Shares hex with ttc-danger — see marketing-decisions.md decision #3. Per that decision, ttc-accent is RESERVED for destructive/error contexts and should NOT be used as a marketing energy color. Use ttc-primary gradients for marketing instead.

tokens.name: ttc-danger
tokens.notes: Destructive / error state. Identical hex to ttc-accent — the two are currently interchangeable in code. Per marketing-decisions.md decision #3, this pairing is owned by destructive semantics; accent-as-marketing is retired.

tokens.name: ttc-red
tokens.notes: Alias of ttc-accent via tailwind.config.js:37. Avoid in new code; prefer ttc-danger for destructive semantics.

tokens.name: ttc-warning
tokens.notes: Warning / caution state. Darker in light mode for AA contrast on cream surface.

tokens.name: ttc-bg
tokens.notes: App-level background. Warm off-black ('Buenos Aires Night') in dark mode, warm cream in light mode — intentionally NOT pure black/white.

tokens.name: ttc-surface
tokens.notes: Elevated surfaces above ttc-bg (sheets, headers).

tokens.name: ttc-card
tokens.notes: Card backgrounds. Identical to ttc-surface in light mode — the elevation is conveyed by border/shadow there.

tokens.name: ttc-card-hover
tokens.notes: Hover state for tappable cards. Identical to ttc-input in light mode.

tokens.name: ttc-border
tokens.notes: Warm neutral border color.

tokens.name: ttc-input
tokens.notes: Form field backgrounds.

tokens.name: ttc-text
tokens.notes: Primary body text. Warm off-white / warm dark brown — intentionally NOT pure white/black.

tokens.name: ttc-text-muted
tokens.notes: Secondary text. In dark mode this is LIGHTER than ttc-text-dim (inverted naming — see brand-audit.md §1 flag #5). Treat 'muted' as 'slightly less prominent than body', not as 'most faded'.

tokens.name: ttc-text-dim
tokens.notes: Tertiary / most-faded text. Naming is inverted vs ttc-text-muted between themes; this token is always the MOST faded text in its respective theme.

tokens.name: ttc-bubble-user-primary
tokens.notes: WhatsApp-style user bubble when Grupos mode (primary/blue) is active. Warm-tinted so it doesn't read as pure blue.

tokens.name: ttc-bubble-user-accent
tokens.notes: WhatsApp-style user bubble when Finanzas mode (accent/green) is active.

tokens.name: ttc-bubble-bot
tokens.notes: Bot reply bubble. Matches ttc-surface (dark) / ttc-input (light).

tokens.name: ttc-toggle-primary
tokens.notes: Active state for Grupos mode toggle. Same hex as ttc-bubble-user-primary by design.

tokens.name: ttc-toggle-accent
tokens.notes: Active state for Finanzas mode toggle. Same hex as ttc-bubble-user-accent by design.

tokens.name: category-food
tokens.notes: Hardcoded in ExpenseItem.vue:149 as a JS map — outside the token system, no dark-mode variant. Orange. Spanish label: 'Comida'.

tokens.name: category-transport
tokens.notes: Hardcoded in ExpenseItem.vue:150. Blue — visually similar to ttc-primary but distinct hex. Spanish label: 'Transporte'.

tokens.name: category-accommodation
tokens.notes: Hardcoded in ExpenseItem.vue:151. Purple. Spanish label: 'Alojamiento'.

tokens.name: category-entertainment
tokens.notes: Hardcoded in ExpenseItem.vue:152. Pink. Spanish label: 'Entretenimiento'.

tokens.name: category-shopping
tokens.notes: Hardcoded in ExpenseItem.vue:153. Emerald green — a FOURTH green in the system, distinct from ttc-secondary / ttc-whatsapp / ttc-success. Flagged as a conflict in brand-audit.md §1. Spanish label: 'Compras'.

tokens.name: category-general
tokens.notes: Hardcoded in ExpenseItem.vue:154. Neutral gray fallback. Spanish label: 'Otro'.

tokens.name: positive-500
tokens.notes: Sentiment scale (50/100/400/500/600/700/900) for AmountDisplay and UserAvatar. Used to color-code positive/credit amounts. Distinct from ttc-secondary — does not theme-switch.

tokens.name: negative-500
tokens.notes: Sentiment scale (50/100/400/500/600/700/900) for AmountDisplay and UserAvatar. Used to color-code negative/debit amounts. Distinct from ttc-danger — does not theme-switch.
